git-foundations.title: Git Foundations
git-foundations.description: Learn Git by building one local repository at a time—from `git init` to a clean, readable history—without remotes, GitHub, or hidden learner setup commands.
git-foundations.moreComingSoon: More Git Foundations lessons are coming soon.

git-foundations-module-1-start-tracking.title: Create Your First Repository
git-foundations-module-1-start-tracking.description: Start with an ordinary folder, initialize Git on main, read its state, and record the first meaningful snapshot.
outcomes: Explain why one project with history is safer than duplicate “final” folders.
Initialize an ordinary folder as a Git repository on main.
Read untracked, tracked, staged, and clean state with Git status.
Stage project files and create a clear first commit.
why: Gives every later Git command a concrete repository to work with.
Builds the inspect, act, verify rhythm used throughout the course.

git-foundations-module-2-meaningful-hist.title: Build Meaningful History
git-foundations-module-2-meaningful-hist.description: Turn related edits into focused commits that another developer can read and inspect.
outcomes: Use git diff to inspect unstaged changes.
Use git add and git diff --staged to prepare one focused snapshot.
Create cohesive commits with meaningful imperative messages.
Read history with git log --oneline and inspect a commit with git show.
why: Turns raw edits into focused snapshots another developer can review.
Makes commit history useful as documentation, not just storage.

git-foundations-module-3-manage-tracked-.title: Manage Tracked Files Safely
git-foundations-module-3-manage-tracked-.description: Keep a repository clean as files are renamed, removed, ignored, or restored.
outcomes: Read how tracked file modifications appear before and after staging.
Rename and remove tracked files with git mv and git rm.
Create and verify .gitignore rules for generated files.
Restore staged and unstaged mistakes safely.
why: Keeps the repository accurate as files change names, disappear, or become generated output.
Builds safe correction habits without rewriting existing history.

git-foundations-module-4-final-capstone.title: Final Capstone
git-foundations-module-4-final-capstone.description: Build and hand off one complete local history for a neighborhood resource guide.
outcomes: Create a clean repository from an ordinary project folder.
Build four purposeful commits whose boundaries match the requested work.
Keep generated output ignored and obsolete files out of HEAD.
Verify the final history and clean working state before handoff.
why: Combines initialization, focused commits, file management, and verification in one realistic handoff.
Produces a complete local history that explains the work from beginning to end.

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: From Folder to First Commit
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Understand the problem Git solves, initialize your first repository, read its state, and move one change into history.

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Module Project
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Build a neighborhood field-notes repository from an ordinary folder to a clean first commit.
bullets: Project: Build the Field Notes Repository

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: From Edits to Understandable Snapshots
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Inspect changes, choose what belongs together, commit one idea at a time, and read the history you created.
bullets: Inspect Unstaged Changes with git diff
Read History with log and show

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Module Project
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Build a readable history for a small community website without mixing unrelated changes.
bullets: Project: Build the Community Website History

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Keep the Repository Honest
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Rename and remove tracked files, ignore generated output, and recover from staging or working-tree mistakes safely.

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Module Project
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Prepare a clean release snapshot while preserving the history that explains how the project changed.
bullets: Project: Prepare a Clean Release Snapshot

git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.title: Final Capstone
git-foundations-git-foundations-section-.description: Build and verify a complete local history for a neighborhood resource guide.
bullets: Final Capstone: Neighborhood Resource Guide History
